checklist.detail: Profitable all 10 of last 10 yrs — verified via FMP
Dividends paid 24+ years (0.40% yield) — verified via yfinance
EPS grew +258.7% over 10 yrs — verified via FMP

ai_analysis.business_context: Apple Inc. operates in Consumer Electronics (Technology). The company has been profitable in 10 of the last 10 years and maintains a 24-year dividend history.
ai_analysis.metrics_assessment: Metrics reflect elevated P/E suggesting stretched valuation, trading well above book value, strong ROE indicating efficient capital use, healthy net margins, liquidity concerns, elevated leverage that could constrain flexibility. Graham score of 4/8 and Buffett score of 3/5 provide quantitative validation of underlying quality.
value_trap_flags: Graham criteria failures: Current Ratio >= 2.0, Debt / Equity <= 1.0; verify these aren't permanent deterioration signals
Buffett competitive moat criteria failures: D/E <= 0.5 (Financial Fortress); business may lack sustainable advantages
Combined liquidity pressure and leverage create refinancing or distress risk
key_signals: Graham framework validates: Adequate Size (Rev > $1B), 10 Yrs Positive Earnings, Dividend Record (20+ yrs)
Buffett quality signals: ROE >= 15% (Competitive Moat), Net Margin >= 10% (Pricing Power)
Dividend aristocrat status (20+ years of payments) suggests stable, mature business with shareholder-friendly management
Superior profitability with both high ROE and margins indicates competitive strength
Consistent profitability through market cycles demonstrates business resilience
disqualifiers: Multiple Graham criteria failures suggest fundamental value screening violations
ai_analysis.recommendation_narrative: Adequate fundamentals (7.5/13 pts) but insufficient checklist validation (4/8 Graham). Quality is present but valuation or execution risk suggests patience for improved entry point.
